stemHtml: A crew is operating a fly-by-wire aircraft which offers protections to ensure stability with regards to aeroplane mass and centre of gravity. If the crew enters the wrong CG location pre-flight, what may happen during take-off?

options.a: The aircraft might become unstable in certain configurations.
options.b: The aircraft will never become unstable due to regulations preventing this.
options.c: Fuel consumption will increase because the aircraft moves the stabiliser to correct for the incorrect CG position.
options.d: Drag increases because the aircraft will try to trim for the actual CG.
